title: hydroleales
intro: Hydroleales is an order of flowering plants that consists of families with unique characteristics, such as the presence of a spathe or bracts that enclose the flower. The order includes a variety of plant families, including Orchidaceae, which comprises orchids, and Euphorbiaceae, which includes spurge and poinsettia. Other families within the Hydroleales order include Cyperaceae, commonly known as sedge and rush families. The diversity of plant forms and growth habits within the Hydroleales order reflects its broad range of adaptations to different environments and ecological niches.

families.family_desc: Hydroleaceae is a family of aquatic plants commonly known as bladderworts, which are carnivorous and found in freshwater environments.
